offer.label: Offer
offer.description: List a service or asset that buyers can purchase

request.label: Request
request.description: Post a task or budget that others can accept

offer.headerDescription: Add an offer title, description, and one or more purchasable items. Delivery opens after the buyer pays.
offer.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this service provides overall.

request.headerDescription: Add a request title, description, and one or more tasks. Delivery opens after someone accepts.
request.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this request needs overall.

project.headerDescription: Add the project name, initial goal, and tasks under that goal. After publishing, continue from the project progress page.
project.goalPlaceholder: Enter the first project goal to move forward
project.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional: describe what this project does in one sentence. If blank, a short overview will be generated.

hints.sellerWallet: Use the X Layer EVM address from your OKX wallet.
hints.difficulty: Higher difficulty estimates a larger Virtual Share release when the task is claimed.
hints.moneyAmount: {label} must be at least 0.01 with up to 2 decimal places
hints.positiveIntegerQuantity: Quantity must be a positive integer
hints.lengthMissing: {label} needs at least {min} characters. Current: {current}; remaining: {remaining}

difficultyHelp.title: How difficulty works
difficultyHelp.description: D1-D5 are quick presets backed by difficulty scores from 0.5 to 8. Higher scores mean more complex tasks and a larger estimated Virtual Share release when claimed.
levels: D1 = 0.5: tiny cleanup, correction, or confirmation task
D3 = 2: medium task with a complete deliverable
D4 = 4: complex implementation, research, or collaboration task
D5 = 8: high-risk core task with high complexity

paymentRule.offer: After the buyer pays, you can submit delivery in the order.
paymentRule.request: Publish the request and wait for acceptance. The fulfiller enters a receiving wallet when accepting.

digitalInventory.defaultDeliveryStandard: After payment, the system automatically reveals one inventory item.
digitalInventory.defaultAcceptanceCriteria: The buyer receives content that matches the listing and can be accessed or used successfully.

items.listDescription: Add multiple {unit}; each one can have its own amount and quantity.

offer.defaultDescription: Inventory defaults to 1. Purchases enter the order delivery flow.
offer.sectionDescription: Buyers choose from these items, and each item has its own price.
offer.deliveryPlaceholder: Example: Deliver one 1024x1024 PNG image with a download link.
offer.acceptancePlaceholder: Example: Complete when the image is clear, watermark-free, and matches the agreed size and style.

request.defaultDescription: Acceptance slots default to 1. Claimed tasks enter the order delivery flow.
request.sectionDescription: Fulfillers choose from these tasks, and each task has its own budget.
request.deliveryPlaceholder: Example: Submit a structured table covering 10 competitors with complete, reviewable fields.
request.acceptancePlaceholder: Example: Complete when fields are complete, source links work, and there are no obvious duplicates or omissions.

project.listDescription: Add one or more tasks. Each task is attached to the initial goal above.
project.defaultDescription: Create the first project task during publishing. Add more tasks on the project detail page later.
project.sectionDescription: Tasks are attached to the initial goal. Difficulty estimates the Virtual Share release when claimed.
project.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional: add background or scope for this task.
project.deliveryPlaceholder: Describe what the claimant needs to deliver.
project.acceptancePlaceholder: Describe what counts as done. Use one line per criterion if needed.

preview.title: Preview
preview.emptyDescription: After you add a description, the listing preview will appear here.
preview.projectEmptyDescription: After you add the initial goal, the project preview will appear here.
preview.projectShares: Claimed tasks release Virtual Shares based on difficulty

submit.readyDescription: After submission, you will land on the detail page. Later transactions are handled in orders.

loginNotice.text: Publishing requires an account. This form will stay on the page after you sign in.

actions.deleteFilledConfirm: This {unit} already has content. Delete it?

errors.loginRequired: Please sign in before publishing. This form will stay on the page.

maxItems.item: You can create up to {count} options while publishing.
maxItems.task: You can create up to {count} tasks while publishing.

errors.minCharsCurrent: {label} must be at least {min} characters; currently {current}
errors.sellerWallet: Enter a valid X Layer EVM receiving address
errors.clipboardReadFailed: Could not read the clipboard. Paste the address manually.
errors.digitalInventoryRequired: Inventory auto-delivery needs at least one inventory item.
errors.inventoryUploadFailed: The listing was published, but inventory upload failed. Add inventory from the detail page management panel before sending buyers to purchase.

workspace.title: Items
workspace.description: The publisher breaks offers into sale options and requests or projects into tasks. Other users pick specific content to create an order.

manage.title: Content management
manage.description: Owner-only maintenance for options and tasks. Buyers or fulfillers still choose from the detail page, so there is only one action entry.

create.offerDescription: Clarify what the buyer receives after purchase and what counts as done.
create.taskDescription: Clarify what the fulfiller delivers and how the publisher accepts it.
create.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional: add background, scope, or notes.
create.deliveryStandardPlaceholder: Describe what the other party receives, such as file format, quantity, link, or documentation.
create.acceptanceCriteriaPlaceholder: Describe what counts as done. Use one line per criterion if needed.
create.agentInstructionPlaceholder: Optional: tell the agent how to execute or package the delivery.

claim.noteHint: Add an execution plan, delivery preference, or anything that needs confirmation.
claim.projectNotePlaceholder: Add execution notes for this task.

actions.closeConfirm: Closing this task stops new claims. Existing orders continue.

edit.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional: add background, scope, or notes.

validation.name: Enter a task name with at least 3 characters.
validation.deliveryStandard: Enter delivery requirements with at least 8 characters.
validation.acceptanceCriteria: Enter at least one completion standard.
validation.difficultyScore: Choose D1-D5 or enter a difficulty score between 0.5 and 8.

reviewed.label: Manual delivery
reviewed.helper: The seller enters delivery content in the order after payment

instant.label: Provider auto-fulfillment
instant.helper: A system provider fulfills after payment; no inventory upload needed

stock.label: Inventory auto-delivery
stock.helper: The platform locks one inventory item and delivers it after payment

directDelivery.heading: Provider auto-fulfillment
directDelivery.retryHint: The order keeps a retry action after a failure.

digitalInventory.heading: Auto-delivery inventory
digitalInventory.createHint: One automatically delivered item per line. The platform encrypts it, then delivers one item after buyer payment.
digitalInventory.editHint: Additional uploads append to the auto-delivery inventory.
digitalInventory.notice: The platform locks one inventory item when the order is created, then delivers it to the buyer after payment succeeds.
digitalInventory.badge: {hasSummary, select, yes {Inventory auto-delivery · available {count}} other {Inventory auto-delivery}}

validation.required: Inventory auto-delivery needs at least one uploaded inventory item.

empty.title: No selectable content yet
empty.ownerDescription: Add an option or task first so others can buy or accept it from the detail page.
empty.viewerDescription: The publisher has not opened specific content yet. Check back later.

toast.itemClosed: Closed. New claims are now disabled.

labels.project: Projects stay open long term. You can update content and tasks only.
labels.offer: Offers can be updated, or closed to stop new purchases.
labels.request: Requests can be updated, or closed to stop new acceptances.

closeConfirm.title: Close this entry point?
closeConfirm.offer: Buyers will no longer be able to purchase this offer. Existing orders continue.
closeConfirm.request: Fulfillers will no longer be able to claim this request. Existing orders continue.

toast.offerClosed: Offer closed. New purchases are now disabled.
toast.requestClosed: Request closed. New claims are now disabled.
